memory.summary: I planned to finish the assignment analysis before reading, but my friends are asking me to join them for lunch now. I need to decide whether to prioritize this social opportunity or stick to my academic plan.
My analysis progress jumped to 0.58, but the group is already ordering; I'll skip lunch to hit my target and avoid wasting this momentum.
I'm at 72% with only an hour left; the risk of submitting incomplete work outweighs skipping another meal. I need to finish this analysis now.
I missed the 18:00 deadline despite hitting 81% progress, and my exhaustion is now critical. I need to accept this failure and shift focus immediately.

I pushed through hunger to salvage the analysis, but my stubborn focus on that metric cost me the reading deadline entirely. Now I'm exhausted and stressed, realizing my short-term drive is destroying my broader responsibilities.
My stubborn push to fix that 0.91 metric cost me the reading deadline entirely, proving my short-term drive is actively destroying my broader responsibilities.
Messaging the cohort feels like a hollow victory; I traded the reading deadline for this 'win' and my stubbornness just deepened the mess.
My stubborn push for a perfect metric just cost me the reading deadline entirely, proving my short-term drive is actively destroying my broader responsibilities.
My stubborn drive to optimize that metric just cost me the reading deadline again; nothing changed because I'm too impulsive to stop before the damage is done.
I'm still stuck in this loop of tiny, inefficient gains while ignoring the bigger picture; my impulsive need to 'do something' is just delaying the inevitable crash.
Closing the chat was necessary; my impulsive need to 'fix' things is costing me more than any metric ever could.
That 0.97 metric feels like a hollow win since I'm completely drained and ignoring the bigger picture again.

desires: Stop the bleeding on the reading deadline before I lose all credibility with Professor prof_assignment.
Force myself to stop optimizing that useless 0.97 metric so I can actually finish the analysis section by tomorrow morning.
Get a nap or sleep immediately because my energy is at zero and I'm about to crash completely.
Reassure Cohort_3 that I haven't dropped the ball on our group work despite my recent self-sabotage.

goals.description: Spend the next 45 minutes aggressively churning through the reading just to hit a visible progress bar, even if I don't retain it. The deadline is in 18 hours; I need to manufacture 'work' now so Professor prof_assignment doesn't think I'm slacking while my energy crashes.
goals.rationale: My achievement drive (0.5 weight) forces me to prioritize output volume over retention quality right now. My impulsive time horizon means I can't afford to wait until tomorrow morning to deal with the professor's expectations. If I look like I've done *something* today, I maintain the facade of competence needed for Cohort_3.

goals.description: Send a quick, confident update to Cohort_3 confirming the analysis section is locked and that the reading is being handled. Do not mention fatigue or the fact that I'm running on empty.
goals.rationale: Assertiveness dictates I control the narrative before they ask. Dropping the ball isn't an option, but explaining why I'm struggling weakens my position. I'll reassure them once the fake progress bar is updated, then cut contact to preserve whatever focus capacity I have left.

agent.last_reasoning: I need to fake progress on the reading before my energy hits zero. This satisfies g3's deadline pressure and protects my achievement metric. I'll ignore the sleep debt cost; if I look busy, Cohort_3 won't question my output quality.

goals.description: Hit the gym for a quick 30-minute sprint to burn off the stress before diving into work.
goals.rationale: My sleep discipline is low (3/10) and I'm already carrying a 2-hour debt; my physical stamina is decent but focus drops fast without movement. As an impulsive achiever, I need immediate dopamine to reset before tackling the 'soft' hardness of the assignment. Skipping this feels like wasting potential energy.

goals.description: Draft the analysis section of the assignment until 18:00 sharp.
goals.rationale: This is non-negotiable. Professor prof_assignment has high trust value, and looking like I'm slacking off violates my core achievement drive. The deadline is hard, so I must prioritize this over leisure right now, even if it means skipping lunch with cohort_3.

goals.description: Skip lunch with cohort_3 today; send a direct text apologizing later.
goals.rationale: I know missing this hurts our high-trust bond, but my current planning horizon is short. I can't juggle the social obligation and the looming academic deadline without risking both. Assertiveness dictates I renegotiate this boundary directly later rather than letting guilt paralyze me now.

desires: Finish the analysis section by 18:00 so I don't look like I'm slacking off to Professor prof_assignment.

action.name: message

args.content: Analysis section is live. My logic holds up better than your excuses. Don't think I'm ignoring you, but I'm done defending myself tonight.

action.reasoning: Submitting aggressively satisfies my achievement drive to prove competence and close the 'win' loop immediately. goals.description: Stop messaging the group chat about the analysis. I've already sent my 'victory lap' message; continuing to defend it now just proves I'm desperate and insecure, not competent.

The 'achievement' drive is screaming to double down on this win, but my 'impulsive' time horizon means I know I'll crash tomorrow if I don't cut the losses now. I need to force a hard boundary: no more validation seeking.
